text: We suffer more often in imagination than in reality.
author: Seneca

text: You have power over your mind — not outside events. Realize this, and you will find strength.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: It is not what happens to you, but how you react to it that matters.
author: Epictetus

text: The unexamined life is not worth living.
author: Socrates

text: Knowing yourself is the beginning of all wisdom.
author: Aristotle

text: Wise men speak because they have something to say; fools because they have to say something.
author: Plato

text: He who has a why to live can bear almost any how.
author: Friedrich Nietzsche

text: Science is organized knowledge. Wisdom is organized life.
author: Immanuel Kant

text: By three methods we may learn wisdom: by reflection, by imitation, and by experience.
author: Confucius

text: The journey of a thousand miles begins with a single step.
author: Lao Tzu

text: No man ever steps in the same river twice.
author: Heraclitus

text: I think, therefore I am.
author: René Descartes

text: Life can only be understood backwards; but it must be lived forwards.
author: Søren Kierkegaard

text: In the midst of winter, I found there was, within me, an invincible summer.
author: Albert Camus

text: Man is condemned to be free.
author: Jean-Paul Sartre

text: A wise man proportions his belief to the evidence.
author: David Hume

text: Peace is a virtue, a state of mind, a disposition for benevolence.
author: Baruch Spinoza

text: Talent hits a target no one else can hit; genius hits a target no one else can see.
author: Arthur Schopenhauer

text: Whereof one cannot speak, thereof one must be silent.
author: Ludwig Wittgenstein

text: The life of man is solitary, poor, nasty, brutish, and short.
author: Thomas Hobbes

text: Whether you think you can, or you think you can't — you're right.
author: Henry Ford

text: The only way to do great work is to love what you do.
author: Steve Jobs

text: Believe you can and you're halfway there.
author: Theodore Roosevelt

text: Do not go where the path may lead, go instead where there is no path and leave a trail.
author: Ralph Waldo Emerson

text: Go confidently in the direction of your dreams. Live the life you have imagined.
author: Henry David Thoreau

text: You may not control all the events that happen to you, but you can decide not to be reduced by them.
author: Maya Angelou

text: The best way to predict the future is to create it.
author: Peter Drucker

text: Success is not final, failure is not fatal: it is the courage to continue that counts.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Whatever you are, be a good one.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: Start where you are. Use what you have. Do what you can.
author: Arthur Ashe

text: It always seems impossible until it's done.
author: Nelson Mandela

text: Fall seven times, stand up eight.
author: Japanese Proverb

text: The secret of getting ahead is getting started.
author: Mark Twain

text: You miss 100% of the shots you don't take.
author: Wayne Gretzky

text: What you get by achieving your goals is not as important as what you become by achieving them.
author: Zig Ziglar

text: The man who moves a mountain begins by carrying away small stones.
author: Confucius

text: Act as if what you do makes a difference. It does.
author: William James

text: Well done is better than well said.
author: Benjamin Franklin

text: If you want to lift yourself up, lift up someone else.
author: Booker T. Washington

text: Motivation is what gets you started. Habit is what keeps you going.
author: Jim Rohn

text: Be the change that you wish to see in the world.
author: Mahatma Gandhi

text: Injustice anywhere is a threat to justice everywhere.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Act only according to that maxim whereby you can will that it should become a universal law.
author: Immanuel Kant

text: Do not do to others what you would not want done to yourself.
author: Confucius

text: Waste no more time arguing about what a good man should be. Be one.
author: Marcus Aurelius

text: The true measure of a man is how he treats someone who can do him no good.
author: Samuel Johnson

text: Nearly all men can stand adversity, but if you want to test a man's character, give him power.
author: Abraham Lincoln

text: No act of kindness, no matter how small, is ever wasted.
author: Aesop

text: Integrity is doing the right thing, even when no one is watching.
author: C.S. Lewis

text: Everyone thinks of changing the world, but no one thinks of changing himself.
author: Leo Tolstoy

text: What is hateful to you, do not do to your fellow.
author: Hillel the Elder

text: It is our choices that show what we truly are, far more than our abilities.
author: J.K. Rowling

text: In matters of style, swim with the current; in matters of principle, stand like a rock.
author: Thomas Jefferson

text: The time is always right to do what is right.
author: Martin Luther King Jr.

text: Where justice is denied, neither persons nor property will be safe.
author: Frederick Douglass

text: Right is right even if no one is doing it; wrong is wrong even if everyone is doing it.
author: Augustine of Hippo

text: A man without ethics is a wild beast loosed upon this world.
author: Albert Camus

text: The measure of a life is not its duration but its donation.
author: Peter Marshall

text: We make a living by what we get, but we make a life by what we give.
author: Winston Churchill

text: Goodness is the only investment that never fails.
author: Henry David Thoreau
